text: Abstract Channel estimation in multi-cell interference-limited cellular systems is studied. Scenarios corresponding to multiple antennas at the transceivers, both for finite and large number of antennas (massive MIMO) are examined. In such systems multi-cell interference is mitigated by using per-cell beamforming at the base-stations. Channel estimation in such systems is a performance bottleneck due to the detrimental pilot contamination effect. We introduce a low-rate coordinated solution to mitigate the pilot contamination impact, using extra information learned from the second-order statistics of the user channels. We show that these extra statistics enable adequate interference differentiation even for heavily correlated pilots. Analytical investigations reveal that in the large antenna array regime (massive MIMO regime), the pilot contamination impact is eliminated under certain conditions on the channel covariance matrices. For smaller antenna array sizes, the proposed scheme provides significant performance gains over conventional channel estimation.
